case_title: ROMULA VILLANUEVA VS. SANTOS A. CADIENTE ET AL

docket_entries.description: ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. *FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY MAY 23, 2018.*

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROMULA VILLANUEVA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SANTO CADIENTE FILED A DECLARATION OF NON-OPPOSITION WITH "CONDITIONS" AND LISTED THREE CONDITIONS THAT HE DESIRED. WHILE THE FIRST AND THIRD CONDITIONS DO NOT APPEAR TO BE CONTROVERSIAL, THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE EITHER THE PURPOSE OF THE SECOND CONDITION OR WHETHER IT IS OPPOSED. SINCE NO EXPLANATION HAS BEEN PROVIDED FOR WHY ANY OF THE THREE CONDITIONS ARE REQUESTED, THE COURT WILL LEAVE IT TO MS. VILLANUEVA WHETHER TO COMPLY WITH THEM. MS. VILLANUEVA MUST FILE HER FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN MAY 23, 2018. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION /Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SONNY CADIENTE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Summary Judgment:

Defendant Sonny Agonoy Cadiente's motion for summary judgment is denied. The claims against the Estate of Decedent Mary B. Agonoy arose at the time of the Decedent's death, not in the conservatorship matter (Case No. PCN-15-299338). Therefore, the representations Plaintiff Romula Villanueva made that she was not a creditor of the estate in the Petitions for conservatorship filed prior to Decedent's death are consistent with Plaintiff's current position as a creditor. (SUMF Nos. 2-3.) As such, there is no basis for judicial or collateral estoppel. Moreover, the Probate Code requires a claim to be filed with the Estate before a separate lawsuit can be instituted. (Probate Code 9000, 9002, 9256; Wilkison v. Wiederkehr (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 822, 828-831.) Plaintiff complied with this procedure and her allegations in the first amended complaint are consistent with her creditor's claim. Res judicata is also inapplicable as Defendant fails to identify a final judgment on the merits of Plaintiff's status as a creditor of the estate.

Plaintiff raises a triable dispute of fact over the adequacy of compensation afforded to her. Plaintiff states that the compensation provided to her was less than the value of the services rendered and that she sacrificed her old job to take care of Decedent. (Villanueva Decl. 2.) Although Plaintiff received an annuity, reimbursement of costs for the conservatorship, and compensation for her caretaker services, there remains a dispute of fact as to whether Plaintiff was fully compensated and whether there was adequate consideration for the promise that was allegedly made to her by Decedent. (UMF Nos. 15-21; AUMF Nos. 1-3; see Byrne v. Laura (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 1054, 1063-1066 [summary adjudication is an inappropriate means of resolving ambiguities regarding contract for support of decedent]; Gomes v. Borba (1950) 99 Cal.App.2d 38, 42 [adequacy of consideration is a matter for the trier of fact].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Amanda Ebey, Tel. No. 1-415-989-8070 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Romula Villanueva's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Counsel for defendant Santo Cadiente filed a declaration of non-opposition with "conditions" and listed three conditions that he desired. While the first and third conditions do not appear to be controversial, the court is unable to determine either the purpose of the second condition or whether it is opposed. Since no explanation has been provided for why any of the three conditions are requested, the court will leave it to Ms. Villanueva whether to comply with them. Ms. Villanueva must file her first amended complaint no later than May 23, 2018. Order signed in open court. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
